edus.text: In a jobs report
that may influence the Federal Reserve 's decision on interest rates ,
the Labor Department says
that 271,000 jobs were added in October .
The unemployment rate fell slightly to 5 percent ,
according to the report from the agency 's Bureau of Labor Statistics .
It 's the biggest one-month jobs gain in all of 2015 ,
according to Bloomberg News ,
which adds
that the strong result is one of the positive signs
the Fed 's economists `` are looking for
as they consider a year-end boost in borrowing costs . ''
Friday 's announcement tops economists ' consensus expectations ,
which had forecast a modest gain of about 180,000 jobs .
Speak
ng in Congress this week ,
ederal Reserve Chair Janet Yellen left open the possibility of an interest rate hike ,
hile also saying
t would be `` very gradual . ''
iting good economic results ,
ellen said
here is a `` live possibility ''
hat policymakers might raise rates
hen the Fed meets in mid-December .
ast month , the Labor Department reported
hat only 142,000 jobs were created in September ,
ith unemployment holding at 5.1 percent .
ith today 's announcement , the BLS said
he job-creation number was being revised to 137,000 .
ctober 's job growth occurred `` in professional and business services , health care , retail trade , food services and drinking places , and construction , ''
he Bureau of Labor Statistics says .
s for wages , the month also brought a 9-cent rise in the average hourly earning rate ,
ith workers on private nonfarm payrolls now making an average of $ 25.20 ,
ccording to the BLS ,
hich adds
hat for the group , `` hourly earnings have risen by 2.5 percent over the year . ''
he agency also says
hat the number of people
ho are `` involuntary part-time workers '' due to reduced hours or the difficulty
f finding a full-time job ``
dged down by 269,000 to 5.8 million in October . ''
